last_item: Migrate — 'Start another' (shown after a terminal migration job) left the whole form pre-filled with the prior run's values: source connection, database/target names, and a checked destructive overwrite flag (med/M).
last_result: SHIPPED as a TARGETED reset (not a full wipe). The footgun: pulling a second DB off the same host, a user could skim past a still-armed 'Replace if exists' and silently drop a database. Fixed across all four Migrate flows (one-db, whole-cluster, cross-panel send/receive). First wrote a full-form reset — restraint critic REJECTED (the source connection is reusable for the next DB off the same host; blanking it adds friction to the likely-next task) and proposed resetting only the destructive flag while keeping the connection. Revised to exactly that: KEEP host/port/user/password/sslmode; CLEAR the per-run fields (database-to-copy, target, cluster exclude) and reset overwrite+confirm+error. Send keeps the connection + clears the one-time session code; receive (no connection) clears its db + the generated code. Exported SingleDBForm; added a test that arms overwrite, runs a job to terminal, clicks 'Start another', and asserts the source host PERSISTS while database/target blank and overwrite disarms. 141 tests. Re-ran the panel on the revised behavior → 4 SHIP (UX heuristics: 'keep infrastructure, clear intent' reads natural + error-prevention win on overwrite; Sam: 'worst case is a harmless re-type, never an accidental overwrite'; Priya: same-source repeat friction gone, flag safely cleared; restraint critic: 'minimal correct fix, nothing left to drop'). Priya's non-blocking nit (cluster exclude is arguably source-stable too) left as-is — re-pulling the same whole cluster is rare. Gates: typecheck OK, 141 tests OK, build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox — snap-confine blocks in-sandbox). Lesson recorded: 'reset the form' splits into reusable-infrastructure (keep) vs per-run intent (clear), and any destructive/armed toggle is must-clear regardless. Next top item: Migrate — the overwrite gate is split across three intent-shifts (checkbox → 'Continue…' button → type-the-name modal) with no inline warning when overwrite is checked (med/M); or the cheaper DatabaseTuning intro-line reassurance (S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
